_comment: Single source of truth for NC Flock/ALPR deployments. Both the Cameras page table and the Near You tool render from this file. Edit here and both update. status = active | fight (being decided) | dropped. county = NC county name without the word 'County'.

communities.note: Crowdsourced count; balance owned by other agencies.
None owned by Durham PD directly.
Legal claims tied to wrongful plate-reader stops (Jasmine Horne).
Paid a $60K settlement after a camera-image misread led to a wrongful arrest.
3,302 searches within months of signing; residents blocked from speaking at a June 2026 meeting.
Sheriff's contract (#25-0364) grew ~5x since a 2021 pilot with no public vote; 2.98M searches logged; DeFlock ILM organizing residents against renewal.
Sheriff Robert Fountain flagged concerns; pulled from agenda for community input (July 2026).
Shares data with 200+ police departments in and out of state.
DeFlock Apex pushing to block renewal; records show data shared with ~1,000 agencies.
Proposed multi-year contract (~$449K).
Among the densest per-capita; a town of ~3,000; federally funded.
Countywide network plus separate city cameras (~$30K/yr).
Paused expansion; transparency portal active.
Campus contract signed 2026; data-sharing concerns raised.
~$48K/yr, funded via federal seizure funds.
Campus cameras searched 1.39M times in 3 months by 3,487 agencies, mostly out of state.
Council called the program "too intrusive" (2026).
Advanced amid public comment.
Commissioners voted 5-0 to defund (July 2026).
Board ended the ~$81.5K contract over "data privacy concerns" (Dec 2025).
Board voted 4-2 to terminate; equipment removed by July 2026.
County voted to pull its own cameras (2026).
